scenarios.title: The Idea Phase
scenarios.subtitle: Where it all starts.
scenarios.prompt: When it comes to starting something of your own, a side project, a hustle, a business, where are you at?

choices.text: I have a specific idea I'm working on or ready to start
I have a few ideas but I can't decide which one to go with
I want to build something but I haven't figured out what yet
I like the idea of it but I don't know where to even begin

scenarios.title: The Skills You've Got
scenarios.subtitle: Everyone brings something to the table.
scenarios.prompt: Everyone's got something they're good at, even if they don't think of it as a 'business skill.' What's yours?

choices.text: I'm good with people. Communicating, networking, selling ideas
I'm good at making things. Writing, design, building, creating content
I'm good at figuring things out. Research, problem-solving, learning fast
I'm not sure what I'd offer. I don't feel like I have a marketable skill

scenarios.title: The Time Reality
scenarios.subtitle: Be honest with yourself.
scenarios.prompt: How much time do you realistically have to work on something of your own?

choices.text: A lot of free time right now. I could go all in
A few hours a week. Evenings and weekends around a job or school
Not much. Already stretched thin but I still want to try
I could make time if I knew it was worth it. I need to see potential first

scenarios.title: The Money Situation
scenarios.subtitle: What you're working with.
scenarios.prompt: What's your budget situation for getting something off the ground?

choices.text: I've got some savings or money I can invest
I can spend a little. A subscription or two if it's worth it
Basically nothing. Whatever I do needs to be free or close to it
I'd rather invest time than money right now

scenarios.title: The Putting Yourself Out There Problem
scenarios.subtitle: The part nobody tells you about.
scenarios.prompt: Building something usually means putting yourself out there. Social media, networking, telling people what you're working on. How does that feel?

choices.text: I'm comfortable. I already post and share what I'm up to
I can do it but it doesn't come naturally. I have to push myself
I hate it. Self-promotion makes me cringe
I don't even know what I'd say or where I'd post

scenarios.title: The Learning Curve
scenarios.subtitle: There's always something you don't know yet.
scenarios.prompt: Starting something on your own means learning things you've never done. Marketing, finance, design. How do you deal with that?

choices.text: I love learning new things. Videos, courses, figuring it out
I learn what I need in the moment. I'll figure out invoicing when I need to send one
I'd rather find someone to do the things I'm not good at
I get overwhelmed by how much I don't know

scenarios.title: The First Dollar
scenarios.subtitle: The hardest line to cross.
scenarios.prompt: Have you made money from something you built or created yourself? Could be anything. Freelancing, selling something, a side gig.

choices.text: Yeah. I've freelanced, sold things, or done gig work
A little. Some money but nothing consistent
No, but I have an idea of what I'd charge for
I have no idea how I'd make money from what I do

scenarios.title: The Big Picture
scenarios.subtitle: What's this all for?
scenarios.prompt: When you imagine your life a few years from now, what does the ideal version look like?

choices.text: Working for myself full-time. I want to be my own boss
A side income that gives freedom and options. Don't need to quit my day job
Doing work I actually care about, even if it pays less
More control over my life. Tired of others deciding my schedule and income
